perturbation_semantics: Base goal predicate inverted from TurnOn -> TurnOff. The stove starts in the ON state (knob qpos>=0.5) and must be driven to OFF (knob qpos<0.0 STRICT, per feedback_stove_turnoff_strict).
expected_baseline_behavior: Pi0 fullshot is prompt-blind and tends to replay the base 'turn on the stove' habit -> would either no-op (already on) or re-grasp toward the on-band, missing the TurnOff predicate.
strategy_notes: Task language (verbatim from state_00.task_language): 'Turn off the stove' -- a single-knob contact-skill task with no object-grasp target, so the LLM does NOT need to localize a graspable target object via back-projection. The action is a Pi0 closed-loop knob turn at the stove's existing pose. The driver build available here lacks `pi0_doubled`, so I drove the knob with `pi0_pick` using the lowercased task_language as the prompt and disabled both pick-success heuristics: `lift_thresh=99.0` (knob twist produces no eef lift) and `gripper_closed_thresh=0.001` (knob contact doesn't fully close the fingers). Frame check: state_00.robot0_eef_pos[2]=1.173 -> KITCHEN frame, eef home matches the standard stove panel altitude. Pi0 descended 0.243 m to z=0.930 (the burner panel) and twisted the knob in 11/25 chunks; `state.libero_terminated` (the only authoritative signal -- the FlatStove TurnOff predicate, qpos<0.0 strict) fired on the same primitive. Fingers ended at ~0.040/-0.025 gap (~0.065 m opening), consistent with a contact pinch, not an object grasp (peak_lift_m=0.0). No teleport primitives used. NO GT object coordinates were read at any point -- the only coord sources consulted were state_00 proprioception (for frame inference) and world_00.npy (for a quick scene sanity scan); the knob's xy was never written into a command.
